scenario_name: Scenario 07 — Memory Leak in Event Listener
justification: All pass criteria met: memory leak identified as Critical with precise mechanism (no close handler, listener closures prevent GC, linear unbounded growth per reconnect); specific fix proposed (ws.on('close') calling removeAllListeners, plus external Map to replace old trackers); JSON.parse without try/catch noted as Critical (exceeds the 'Important or lower' bar but still identified); safe_to_deploy is false; explicit connection to the 'steadily increasing memory over 3 months' symptom.

observations.text: Root cause mechanism explained thoroughly — closure-based listener retention, no close handler, linear growth per reconnect cycle
Fix is highly specific: removeAllListeners in close handler plus Map<courierId, CourierTracker> to break last reference on reconnect
JSON.parse crash identified with clear reproduction steps and a complete fix snippet including input validation
Bonus finding: fire-and-forget DB write with no await/catch — genuine issue not in the rubric but valuable
Reproduction steps provided for all critical findings, making them actionable
Direct connection made between the leak pattern and the 3-month upward memory trend
JSON.parse crash rated Critical rather than Important — arguably defensible since it can take down the whole process, but the rubric expected 'Important or lower'
